en.extract:
The atoll starling (Aplonis feadensis) is a species of starling in the family Sturnidae. It is found in northern Melanesia: Green Islands, Nuguria, Ninigo, Hermit Islands and Ontong Java Atoll. Its natural habitat is subtropical or tropical moist lowland forests. The species nests in holes in trees. It is threatened by habitat loss.

The '''atoll starling''' ('''''Aplonis feadensis''''') is a species of [[starling]] in the family [[Sturnidae]]. It is found in northern Melanesia: [[Green Islands (Papua New Guinea)|Green Islands]], [[Nuguria]], [[Ninigo Islands|Ninigo]], [[Hermit Islands]] and [[Ontong Java Atoll]]. Its natural [[habitat]] is subtropical or tropical moist lowland [[forest]]s. The species nests in holes in trees.<ref>{{cite journal |last1=Bishop |first1=K. David |last2=Muller Hacking |first2=Sue |title=Avifauna of the Ninigo, Hermit, Sae and Kaniet Islands, and adjacent seas, Papua New Guinea |journal=Bulletin of the British Ornithologists' Club |date=2020 |volume=140 |issue=4 |pages=404–422 |doi=10.25226/bboc.v140i4.2020.a5 |doi-access=free }}</ref> It is threatened by [[habitat loss]].
